mapping.b_element: the charged scalar fields (condensate order parameters) of the Abelian Higgs model

mapping.a_element: two pairing channels / FFLO-modulated order parameter (s-wave vs d-wave)
mapping.b_element: the two charged scalar fields of the two-component condensate

mapping.a_element: internal magnetic field structure around the vortex
mapping.b_element: the vortex magnetic field concentrated in a 'pipe' off-center
geometric center of the pipelike vortex

brief_justification: The overlap is only at the object level — both study multi-component superconducting/condensate vortices with structured magnetic fields — but the machinery differs decisively: paper A solves the microscopic quasiclassical Eilenberger Green's-function equations self-consistently to obtain quasiparticle spectra (local density of states), pair potentials, and paramagnetic response, whereas paper B performs a classical Bogomol'nyi/BPS analysis of topological soliton solutions in a two-component Abelian Higgs gauge theory (energy bounds, winding numbers, longitudinal currents). Fermionic quasiclassical LDOS calculation and classical energy-minimizing field-theory solitons are different governing frameworks, so no role-for-role method mapping holds.
